coreIssue: This growth conversation is about whether Ahmed defines Head of Product growth through strategic ownership and influence, or keeps anchoring his value in tactical delivery.
openingQuestion: What kind of strategic product ownership would you want to handle with more confidence to grow beyond tactical delivery in your Head of Product scope?
listenFor: whether he names one end-to-end product area he should own without dropping into task-level detail
whether he pivots quickly into sprint execution, backlog specifics, or delivery firefighting when asked about senior scope
if they name specific stakeholders whose trust or visibility would change his ability to move decisions
avoid: do not turn this into a critique of his current execution style before you hear how he defines senior Head of Product scope
do not leave with a broad list of leadership habits instead of one strategic ownership area tied to influence at his level
goodOutcome: You and Ahmed have agreed one Head of Product-level scope he will own more end-to-end, plus the stakeholder influence expected for that ownership to count as senior-level growth.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent moment where Ahmed stayed in tactical delivery on a topic that likely needed Head of Product-level framing, so you can test whether it was pressure or role definition.
